pain-relief.label: Pain Relief
pain-relief.description: Strains targeting CB1, CB2, and TRPV1 pain receptors with anti-inflammatory terpene profiles.
pain-relief.science: Caryophyllene (CB2 agonist), myrcene (GABA-A potentiator), and THC (CB1/mu-opioid) work synergistically to modulate pain signaling. CBD adds allosteric CB1 modulation. (Russo 2011, Ferber et al. 2020)

sleep.label: Sleep & Insomnia
sleep.description: Sedating strains rich in myrcene and CBN, targeting GABA-A and adenosine pathways.
sleep.science: Myrcene potentiates GABA-A receptors and enhances blood-brain barrier permeability. Linalool adds independent GABAergic sedation. CBN is the strongest sleep-specific cannabinoid. (Surendran et al. 2021)

anxiety-safe.label: Anxiety-Safe
anxiety-safe.description: Low-anxiety strains with linalool and CBD — gentle on 5-HT1A serotonin receptors.
anxiety-safe.science: Linalool directly activates 5-HT1A serotonin receptors and potentiates GABA-A. CBD is a potent 5-HT1A agonist (Ki≈16nM). Low THC avoids biphasic anxiogenesis. (Blessing et al. 2015, Linck et al. 2010)

giggles.label: Laughter & Giggles
giggles.description: Dopamine-boosting strains with limonene and terpinolene for euphoric, giggly highs.
giggles.science: Limonene and THC stimulate dopamine D2 receptor release, producing euphoria and spontaneous laughter. Terpinolene adds an uplifting, cerebral dimension. (Komiya et al. 2006)

creativity.label: Creative Flow
creativity.description: Strains activating dopamine and serotonin pathways for divergent thinking.
creativity.science: Pinene inhibits acetylcholinesterase (preserving acetylcholine for working memory). Limonene boosts dopamine. Moderate THC increases divergent thinking via frontal lobe disinhibition. (Schafer et al. 2012)

energy-focus.label: Energy & Focus
energy-focus.description: Stimulating strains rich in pinene and limonene for alertness, motivation, and sustained focus via norepinephrine, TRPV1, and AChE pathways.
energy-focus.science: Pinene inhibits acetylcholinesterase, preserving acetylcholine for sustained attention and working memory. Limonene modulates norepinephrine release for motivation. THCV provides short-acting stimulation without sedative tail. Combined D1 receptor activation supports cognitive performance. (Russo 2011, Perry et al. 2000)

appetite.label: Appetite Stimulation
appetite.description: Strains activating CB1 and ghrelin receptors to boost appetite.
appetite.science: THC directly activates hypothalamic CB1 receptors, increasing ghrelin release and enhancing food palatability. Myrcene adds comfort. Higher THC produces stronger appetite stimulation. (Kirkham et al. 2002)

relaxation.label: Deep Relaxation
relaxation.description: Myrcene-rich strains for CB1 and GABA-A mediated full-body relaxation.
relaxation.science: Myrcene potentiates GABAergic transmission and increases blood-brain barrier permeability for faster cannabinoid onset. Caryophyllene activates CB2 for peripheral relaxation without cognitive impairment. (Russo 2011)

social.label: Social & Conversation
social.description: Strains boosting dopamine and GABA for talkative, social highs.
social.science: Limonene elevates mood via serotonin receptor modulation. Moderate THC reduces social inhibition through amygdala suppression. Terpinolene adds gentle euphoria without sedation. (de Almeida et al. 2012)

euphoria.label: Euphoria & Mood Lift
euphoria.description: THC and limonene-rich strains targeting CB1 and dopamine D2 for mood elevation.
euphoria.science: THC activates mesolimbic dopamine release via CB1 receptors in the ventral tegmental area. Limonene provides independent serotonergic mood elevation. Higher THC generally produces stronger euphoria. (Covey et al. 2015)
